title: Adult Influenza Vaccination
text: Annual influenza vaccination is recommended for all persons aged 6 months or older who do not have contraindications. For adults 65 years or older, ACIP preferentially recommends the use of a higher-dose or adjuvanted influenza vaccine when available. Persons with egg allergy of any severity may receive any age-appropriate influenza vaccine.

title: Tdap and Td Booster in Adults
text: Adults who have not previously received Tdap should receive 1 dose of Tdap, followed by Td or Tdap every 10 years. Pregnant persons should receive 1 dose of Tdap during each pregnancy, preferably during the early part of gestational weeks 27 through 36, regardless of prior vaccination history.

title: Pneumococcal Vaccination in Adults
text: For pneumococcal-naive adults 65 years or older, ACIP recommends either a single dose of PCV20, or a single dose of PCV15 followed by PPSV23 at least 1 year later (≥8 weeks for immunocompromised). Adults aged 19 to 64 with chronic medical conditions (including diabetes, chronic heart, lung, liver, or kidney disease) or immunocompromising conditions should also receive pneumococcal vaccination using one of the same options.

title: Recombinant Zoster Vaccine (Shingrix) in Adults
text: ACIP recommends 2 doses of recombinant zoster vaccine (RZV, Shingrix) separated by 2 to 6 months for the prevention of herpes zoster and its complications in immunocompetent adults 50 years or older, and in immunocompromised adults 19 years or older. RZV is preferred over the older live zoster vaccine and should be given regardless of prior zoster history or prior receipt of the live vaccine.

title: COVID-19 Vaccination in Adults
text: ACIP recommends that all persons 6 months and older receive an updated 2024-2025 COVID-19 vaccine. Adults 65 years or older are recommended to receive a second updated dose at least 4 months after the first. Moderately or severely immunocompromised persons should receive an additional updated dose at least 2 months after the most recent dose, and may benefit from further doses based on clinical judgment.
